title: ANN algorithms: HNSW, IVF, and PQ
description: How graph-based HNSW, inverted-file IVF, and product quantization trade recall, memory, and build cost.
body: Use when choosing an approximate index structure. HNSW builds a navigable small-world graph giving high recall at low latency but high memory; IVF partitions vectors into clusters and probes a subset (nprobe) trading recall for speed; product quantization compresses vectors to cut memory at some recall cost. Do not assume one fits all — memory, recall, and update patterns differ. Actions: pick HNSW for recall-critical in-memory search, IVF/PQ when memory-bound, and benchmark recall/latency. Validation signals include a recall-vs-memory comparison across structures and a chosen index meeting the budget. Risk boundary: PQ's compression can quietly cap recall; if quantized recall misses the floor, escalate to a less compressed index.
